top_signals.title: French Socialists Oppose Moulin as Bank of France Head
top_signals.summary: Socialist lawmakers will oppose Emmanuel Macron’s nominee for Bank of France governor, raising the risks of a political blow for the French president and uncertainty over the composition of the European Central Bank’s Governing Council.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Sinomine Seeks $760 Million for African Lithium, Copper Projects
top_signals.summary: China’s Sinomine Resource Group Co. is seeking as much as 5.2 billion yuan ($764 million) to fund projects in Africa, as it steps up investments in raw materials critical to the energy transition.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Long Bond Yield Hits Highest Since 2007 on Inflation Concern
top_signals.summary: Yields on the longest-dated US Treasuries rose to the highest level in almost two decades as investor concern over accelerating inflation fueled a selloff in global debt markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Republic of the Marshall Islands Recognized for 22 Consecutive Years of QUALSHIP 21
top_signals.summary: The Republic of the Marshall Islands (RMI) Registry’s long-term and consistent commitment to shipping continues to be recognized by the international community. The recently released United States Coast Guard (USCG) 2025 Port State Control (PSC)…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
